records.ety: [L. pragmaticus busy, active, skilled in business, especially in law and state affairs, systematic, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ a thing done, business, fr. <?/ to do: cf. F. pragmatique. See Practical.]

defs: Of or pertaining to business or to affairs; of the nature of business; practical; material; businesslike in habit or manner.
quotes: The next day . . . I began to be very pragmatical.
We can not always be contemplative, diligent, or pragmatical, abroad; but have need of some delightful intermissions.
Low, pragmatical, earthly views of the gospel.

defs: Busy; specifically, busy in an objectionable way; officious; fussy and positive; meddlesome.
quotes: The fellow grew so pragmatical that he took upon him the government of my whole family.

defs: Philosophical; dealing with causes, reasons, and effects, rather than with details and circumstances; -- said of literature.
